ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Proof Of Payment Of Attorney'S Fees.
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Continued To Apr-19-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-13-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lovely Sindhu, via Zoom, in propria persona, interested Party.

The court confirms with Ms. Sindhu that her mother injured herself and is unable to attend the hearing this morning. The court and Ms. Sindhu discuss the identity of the decedent's father. Ms. Sindhu is sworn-in by the clerk, in open court. Ms. Sindhu confirms that Davinder Singh Sidhu's biological father is Satnam Singh Sidhu and that she has filed an application with the California Department of Public Health's vital records to amend the death certificate to make the correction. The court and Ms. Sindhu discuss possible fees pending to the former attorney's estate with Ms. Sindhu stating that she does not believe there are any outstanding attorney's fees. The court advises that it intends to order an amount to the attorney be set aside for six (6) months or longer in the event someone files a claim in this matter for attorney's fees.

The Order to Appear is taken off calendar. The Accounting Petition is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Continued To Aug-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to in propria persona Administrator Harbans Sidhu to appear Aug-30-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and to explain why a corrected death certificate has not been filed, and to explain why admissible evidence demonstrating that Satnam Sigh Sidhu is the decedent's father and not the man named on his death certificate, has not been provided to the court. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-31-2022 Continued To Jul-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Continued To May-31-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-09-2022 Continued To Apr-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Harbans Sidhu appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Gursharan Sidhu (Harbans Daughter) also appeared by zoom.

The court noted that the attorney has passed away. Gursharan stated that they have really struggled to find a lawyer, but she thinks they may have found somebody who can take this on for them (R. Dustin MacFarland out of Sacramento), that she needs to call him today and get the paper work started, and requested an extension. Gursharan also stated that she does not know if the deceased attorney has a practice administrator and provided her e-mail address (lvlysidhu@yahoo.com). The court raised the issue of needing to correct/clarify the father's name, and noted that the death certificate shows the father as Harvant Singh Rattanpal and the petition shows the father as Satnam Singh Sidhu. Gursharan stated that Harvant is the stepfather and that Satnam is the biological father. The court stated that this needs to be reconciled/corrected and advised Gursharan that she needs to make sure she points this out to the new attorney. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to April 6, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A substitution of attorney should be in by then.
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-15-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-15-2021 Continued To Feb-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Harbans Sidhu, via ZOOM.

Ms. Sidhu's daughter advises the court that her mom's attorney has passed away. The court asks if her mom intends to seek new counsel. The daughter isn't clear if that is the plan and suggests that her mom may be able to continue with this matter in propria persona. The court suggests to the daughter that her mother consider hiring another attorney to assist her.

After additional discussion, the court advises Ms. Sidhu that it intends send her a list of what the current issues in this case are via email, so that she can forward the list to the new attorney, if needed.

The Accounting Petition is continued to Feb-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear is off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-17-2021 Continued To Dec-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Administrator Harbans Kaur Sidhu and Attorney Amanda R. Hall to appear on Dec-15-2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear, and why the instant petition should not be taken off calendar, and why there has been no progress in this matter, and why there has been no response to the Examiner's notes. The hearing on the Accounting Petition is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Continued To Sep-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Muller, Esq., counsel for Harbans Sidhu, personal representative.

At the Court's invitation, Ms. Muller advises the Court of certain events that have delayed the filing of the Petition but represents to the court that the petition is in the mail. Ms. Hilton notes that the Petition is such that it is required to be e-filed. After further discussion concerning the cost of the filing, Ms. Muller advises that she will re-file the Petition electronically. The Court continues the matter to September 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204'

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody; Examiner: Sandra Hilton; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not
Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (D204)
